basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: • Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/Utimaco%20Enterprise%20Secure%20Key%20Manager/ReleaseNotes.md) • There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The [Utimaco ESKM](https://utimaco.com/key-management/enterprise-secure-key-manager) solution ingests KMIP server logs from Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager (ESKM) into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Platform (CCP). It enables monitoring of key management operations, authentication events, and KMIP client activity, helping you detect misuse, configuration issues, and unauthorized access to cryptographic material. **Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:** a. [Microsoft Sentinel Codeless Connector Platform (CCP)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/create-codeless-connector) b. [Azure Monitor Data Collection Rules and Endpoints](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/data-collection-rule-overview) **Data Connectors:** 1, **Workbooks:** 1, **Analytic Rules:** 3, **Hunting Queries:** 4 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

basics.name: workspace
basics.label: Workspace
basics.toolTip: This dropdown will list only workspace that exists in the Resource Group selected

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager (ESKM). You can get Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager (ESKM) data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: This solution installs workbook(s) to help you gain insights into the telemetry collected in Microsoft Sentinel. After installing the solution, start using the workbook in Manage solution view.
This solution installs the following analytic rule templates. After installing the solution, create and enable analytic rules in Manage solution view.

elements.name: analytic1
elements.label: Utimaco ESKM - Multiple KMIP authentication failures from same IP

options.text: This query searches for 5 or more KMIP "Authentication Failure" events originating from the same client IP within a 15 minute window on Utimaco ESKM. This may indicate brute-force or credential-stuffing activity against the KMIP server.

elements.name: analytic2
elements.label: Utimaco ESKM - PERMISSION_DENIED burst for a KMIP user

options.text: Identifies a single KMIP user receiving 10 or more PERMISSION_DENIED responses within 30 minutes on Utimaco ESKM. May indicate privilege probing, key access reconnaissance, or a misconfigured application.

elements.name: analytic3
elements.label: Utimaco ESKM - Burst of KMIP DESTROY operations by a single user

options.text: Identifies a single KMIP user issuing 20 or more DESTROY operations within 10 minutes on Utimaco ESKM. May indicate mass key/object deletion, insider sabotage, or ransomware-style cryptographic-material destruction.
This solution installs the following hunting queries. After installing the solution, run these hunting queries to hunt for threats in Manage solution view.

elements.name: huntingquery1
elements.label: Utimaco ESKM - Rare KMIP users in the last 24 hours

options.text: Identifies KMIP user accounts seen in the last 24 hours that were not observed in the prior 14 days. Rare accounts may indicate newly provisioned credentials or unauthorized integrations. This hunting query depends on UtimacoESKMConnector data connector (UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery2
elements.label: Utimaco ESKM - New source IPs connecting to KMIP

options.text: Identifies client IPs connecting to Utimaco ESKM in the last 24 hours that were not observed in the prior 14 days. New source IPs against a key management appliance can indicate a newly onboarded service, a misconfigured client, or an unauthorized application reaching cryptographic material. This hunting query depends on UtimacoESKMConnector data connector (UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery3
elements.label: Utimaco ESKM - High-volume private key retrievals by user

options.text: Surfaces users issuing an unusually high number of KMIP GET / EXPORT operations against PRIVATE_KEY objects on Utimaco ESKM over the last 24 hours. Bulk retrieval of private keys may indicate credential harvesting or pre-exfiltration staging of cryptographic material. This hunting query depends on UtimacoESKMConnector data connector (UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL Parser or Table)

Surfaces KMIP operations performed against Utimaco ESKM outside typical business hours (configurable; defaults to 20:00-06:00 UTC and weekends). Useful for hunting opportunistic or insider activity that deliberately avoids working-hours monitoring. Tune the time window and weekday filter for your environment. This hunting query depends on UtimacoESKMConnector data connector (UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL Parser or Table)
